memberdef.type: std::function< void(const std::string &k, const Buffer &v, const std::string &cid)>
memberdef.definition: using sgns::crdt::CrdtSet::PutHookPtr = std::function<void( const std::string &k, const Buffer &v, const std::string &cid )>
memberdef.name: PutHookPtr

detaileddescription.para:
Function pointer to notify caller if key added to datastore
k
key name
v
buffer value

memberdef.definition: using sgns::crdt::CrdtSet::DeleteHookPtr = std::function<void( const std::string &k, const std::string &cid )>
memberdef.name: DeleteHookPtr

detaileddescription.para:
Function pointer to notify caller when key deleted from datastore
k
key name

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< DataStore > aDatastore, const HierarchicalKey &aNamespace, PutHookPtr aPutHookPtr=nullptr, DeleteHookPtr aDeleteHookPtr=nullptr)
memberdef.name: CrdtSet

detaileddescription.para:
Constructor
aDatastore
Pointer to datastore
aNamespace
Namespce key (e.g "/namespace")
aPutHookPtr
Function pointer to nofify when key added to datastore, default nullptr
aDeleteHookPtr
Function pointer to nofify when key deleted from datastore, default nullptr
Copy constructor
Destructor

Equality operator
true if equal otherwise, it returns false.

Equality operator
true if NOT equal otherwise, it returns false.
Assignment operator

Get value from datastore for HierarchicalKey defined
aKey
HierarchicalKey to get value from datastore
buffer value as string or outcome::failure on error

Returns a new delta-set removing the given keys with prefix /namespace/s/key
aKey
delta key to remove from datastore
pointer to delta or outcome::failure on error

Get the value of an element from the CRDT set /namespace/k/key/v
aKey
Key name
buffer value or outcome::failure on error

Query datastore key-value pairs by prefix, if prefix empty return all elements /namespace/k/prefix
aPrefix
prefix to search, if empty string, return all
aSuffix
suffix to search
list of key-value pairs matches prefix and suffix
QuerySuffix

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &prefix_base, const std::string &middle_part, const std::string &remainder_prefix, const QuerySuffix &aSuffix=QuerySuffix::QUERY_ALL) const
memberdef.name: QueryElements

briefdescription.para: Queries with a middle part that can be a wildcard, negated string or normal string.

parameterdescription.para: The remainder part of the query prefix

detaileddescription.para:
Returns true if the key belongs to one of the elements in the /namespace/k/key/v set, and this element is not tombstoned.
aKey
key name
true if the key belongs to one of the elements and is not tombstoned or outcome::failure on error

Returns in we have a key/block combinations in the elements set that has not been tombstoned.
aKey
key name
true if the key has not been tombstoned, false otherwise or outcome::failure on error

Get full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/key
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with key prefix

Get elems full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/s/key
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with elems prefix

Get tombs full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/t/key
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with tombs prefix

Get keys full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/k/key
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with key prefix

Get value full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/k/key/v
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with value prefix

Get priority full path prefix in namespace for a key /namespace/k/key/p
aKey
key string
HierarchicalKey with priority prefix

Get priority for a key from datastore
aKey
key string
priority of the key or outcome::failure on error

Set priority for a key and put into datastore
aKey
key string
aPriority
priority to save
priority of the key or outcome::failure on error

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &aKey, const std::string &aID, const Buffer &aValue, uint64_t aPriority)
memberdef.name: SetValue

detaileddescription.para:
Sets a value to datastore if priority is higher. When equal, it sets if the value is lexicographically higher than the current value.
aKey
key string
aID
tomb key ID
aValue
buffer value to set
aPriority
priority to save
priority of the key or outcome::failure on error

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::unique_ptr< storage::BufferBatch > &aDataStore, const std::string &aKey, const std::string &aID, const Buffer &aValue, uint64_t aPriority)
memberdef.name: SetValue

detaileddescription.para:
Sets a value to datastore in batch mode if priority is higher. When equal, it sets if the value is lexicographically higher than the current value.
aDataStore
datastore batch
aKey
key string
aID
tomb key ID
aValue
buffer value to set
aPriority
priority to save
priority of the key or outcome::failure on error

putElems adds items to the "elems" set. It will also set current values and priorities for each element. This needs to run in a lock, as otherwise races may occur when reading/writing the priorities, resulting in bad behaviours.

Technically the lock should only affect the keys that are being written, but with the batching optimization the locks would need to be hold until the batch is written), and one lock per key might be way worse than a single global lock in the end.
aElems
list of elems to to into datastore
aID
tomb key ID
aPriority
priority to save
outcome::success on success or outcome::failure otherwise

PutTombs adds items to the "tombs" set (marked as deleted)
aTombs
list of tomb elements to put into datastore
aID
tomb key ID
outcome::success on success or outcome::failure otherwise

Merge elems and tombs from delta into datastore
aDelta
delta with elems and tombs to save into datastore
aID
tomb key ID
outcome::success on success or outcome::failure otherwise

Check if key is tombstoned with tomb ID and found in datastore
aKey
key string
aID
tomb key ID
true if key with ID is tombstoned, false otherwise or outcome::failure on error

The PutHook function is triggered whenever an element is successfully added to the datastore (either by a local or remote update), and only when that addition is considered the prevalent value.
putHookPtr
Function pointer to callback function

The DeleteHook function is triggered whenever a version of an element is successfully removed from the datastore (either by a local or remote update). Unordered and concurrent updates may result in the DeleteHook being triggered even though the element is still present in the datastore because it was re-added. If that is relevant, use Has() to check if the removed element is still part of the datastore.
deleteHookPtr
Function pointer to callback function

Perform a Sync against all the paths associated with a key prefix
aKeyList
all heads and the set entries related to the given prefix
outcome::success on success or outcome::failure otherwise
Get priority suffix
Get value suffix

Returns a new delta-set adding the given key/value.
aKey
delta key to add to datastore
aValue
delta value to add to datastore
pointer to new delta or outcome::failure on error

briefdescription.para: CrdtSet implements an Add-Wins Observed-Remove Set using delta-CRDTs (https://arxiv.org/abs/1410.2803) and backing all the data in a datastore. It is fully agnostic to MerkleCRDTs or the delta distribution layer. It chooses the Value with most priority for a Key as the current Value. When two values have the same priority, it chooses by alphabetically sorting their unique IDs alphabetically.
